title: Breynia macrocalyx
taxonomy: Breynia macrocalyx is classified within the family Phyllanthaceae, which is a family of flowering plants. This family is part of the larger order Malpighiales in the clade Rosidae. The family Phyllanthaceae is further grouped into the clade Eurosidae. Breynia macrocalyx is native to Australia, and its classification provides insight into its evolutionary relationships with other plants. Its classification also highlights the diversity of plant life within the Phyllanthaceae family.

intro: Breynia macrocalyx, commonly known as the 'Purple Passion Plant', is a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. Traditionally used in folk medicine, Breynia macrocalyx has been reported to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antidiabetic properties, which may aid in the treatment of various diseases. The bioactive constituents of Breynia macrocalyx include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Breynia macrocalyx is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often consumed to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
